title: GrandCentral Taps: Dominance issues when tapping into/through 'when' regions
body:
Found while working on #4323, tapping across/into 'when' blocks doesn't always work:

Test case, **wiring-xmr.fir**:
```firrtl
circuit Top : %[[
  {
    "class":"sifive.enterprise.grandcentral.DataTapsAnnotation",
    "keys":[
      {
        "class":"sifive.enterprise.grandcentral.ReferenceDataTapKey",
        "source":"~Top|Top/mid:Middle/l1:Leaf1>val",
        "sink":"~Top|Top/mid:Middle/l2:Leaf2>tapL1"
      },
      {
        "class":"sifive.enterprise.grandcentral.ReferenceDataTapKey",
        "source":"~Top|Top/mid:Middle/l2:Leaf2>val",
        "sink":"~Top|Top/mid:Middle/l1:Leaf1>tapL2"
      },
      {
        "class":"sifive.enterprise.grandcentral.ReferenceDataTapKey",
        "source":"~Top|Top/mid:Middle/l1:Leaf1>val",
        "sink":"~Top|Top>tapL1"
      },
      {
        "class":"sifive.enterprise.grandcentral.ReferenceDataTapKey",
        "source":"~Top|Top/mid:Middle/l2:Leaf2>val",
        "sink":"~Top|Top>tapL2"
      }
    ]
  }
]]
  module Leaf1 :
    input c2: UInt<1>

    wire val : UInt<3>
    val <= UInt(1)

    ; Tap Leaf2's val
    when c2:
      wire tapL2 : UInt
      tapL2 is invalid

  module Leaf2 :
    input c1: UInt<1>

    wire val : UInt<3>
    val <= UInt(2)

    ; Tap Leaf1's val
    when c1:
      wire tapL1 : UInt<4>
      tapL1 is invalid

  module Middle :
    input c1: UInt<1>
    input c2: UInt<1>
    
    when c1:
      inst l1 of Leaf1
      l1.c2 <= c2

    when c2:
      inst l2 of Leaf2
      l2.c1 <= c1

  module Top :
    input c1: UInt<1>
    input c2: UInt<1>
    output o: UInt

    ; Tap Leaf1's val
    when c1:
      wire tapL1 : UInt<4>
      tapL1 is invalid
      o <= tapL1

    inst mid of Middle
    mid.c1 <= c1
    mid.c2 <= c2

    ; Tap Leaf2's val
    when c2:
      wire tapL2 : UInt
      tapL2 is invalid
      o <= tapL2
```

We handle this better on main than in that PR presently (which is why I'm looking into it), but we should handle these or be clear about limitations of what we do support.

Errors from recent firtool:

```
wiring-xmr.fir:55:7: error: operand #1 does not dominate this use
      inst l1 of Leaf1
      ^
wiring-xmr.fir:55:7: note: see current operation: "firrtl.connect"(%0#2, %0#2) : (!firrtl.ref<uint<3>>, !firrtl.ref<uint<3>>) -> ()
wiring-xmr.fir:59:7: note: operand defined here (op is neither in a parent nor in a child region)
      inst l2 of Leaf2
      ^
wiring-xmr.fir:62:10: error: operand #1 does not dominate this use
  module Top :
         ^
wiring-xmr.fir:62:10: note: see current operation: "firrtl.connect"(%3, %2) : (!firrtl.uint<4>, !firrtl.uint<3>) -> ()
wiring-xmr.fir:62:10: note: operand defined here (op in a parent region)
```
